title: varronia globosa subsp. humilis
intro: Varronia globosa subsp. humilis, also known as the shrub that has various common names such as "black olive tree" or "shrubby black olive", is a plant species in the family Boraginaceae, native to tropical Africa. It is traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities, making it effective in treating various health conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and skin infections. The health benefits of Varronia globosa subsp. humilis include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and exhibit antioxidant properties. Varronia globosa subsp. humilis can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in traditional medicine in various forms, such as a paste or powder.
